description: Prop firm compliance dashboard — backend engine, dashboard widget, dedicated /prop page, Monte Carlo simulator, and AI integration

userStories.title: Audit Existing Utilities for Prop Compliance
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to audit existing code before implementing prop compliance so that we reuse utilities and avoid duplication.
acceptanceCriteria: Search src/lib/analytics/ for existing equity curve, drawdown, and risk functions
Search src/lib/trades/calculations.ts for P&L utilities
Search src/components/dashboard/widgets/ for existing prop-aware code (e.g., DailyLossProgress)
Search src/components/analytics/ for RiskGauge, MonteCarloChart patterns
Search src/server/api/routers/accounts.ts for getStats and existing prop queries
Search src/server/api/routers/analytics.ts for getRiskMetrics and ruinThreshold handling
Document findings in scripts/ralph/progress.txt: existing utilities to reuse (with file:line), local helpers to extract (with file:line), new utilities needed
Typecheck passes (bun run check)

userStories.title: Create Prop Compliance Constants
userStories.description: As a developer, I want shared constants for prop compliance thresholds and labels so that they're reusable across backend and frontend.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/constants/prop.ts with: COMPLIANCE_STATUS enum/object (safe | caution | danger), COMPLIANCE_THRESHOLDS (safe >30% buffer, caution 10-30%, danger <10%), COMPLIANCE_STATUS_COLORS mapping to Terminal design colors (text-profit, text-primary, text-loss), CHALLENGE_STATUS_LABELS, DRAWDOWN_TYPE_LABELS, PROP_ACCOUNT_TYPES
Export from src/lib/constants/index.ts
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)

userStories.title: Create Prop Compliance Calculation Utilities
userStories.description: As a developer, I want shared utility functions for computing prop compliance metrics so that backend and frontend can use them consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/analytics/prop-compliance.ts with pure calculation functions
calculateDrawdownStatus(currentDrawdownPercent, maxDrawdownPercent) returns { percent, limit, used, remaining, status } respecting COMPLIANCE_THRESHOLDS
calculateDailyPnl(trades, date?) sums netPnl for trades on a given date
calculateDailyLossStatus(dailyPnl, dailyLossLimit, initialBalance) returns { current, limit, used, remaining, status }
calculateProfitTargetProgress(totalPnl, profitTarget, initialBalance) returns { current, target, progress, status }
calculateConsistencyMetric(dailyPnls, consistencyRule) returns { maxDayPercent, limit, isCompliant }
calculateTradingDays(trades) returns { daysTraded, minRequired, remaining }
calculateTrailingDrawdown(equityCurve, initialBalance) computes high-water-mark drawdown from equity curve
calculateStaticDrawdown(initialBalance, currentBalance) straightforward formula
getOverallComplianceStatus(metrics[]) returns worst status across all metrics
Export from src/lib/analytics/index.ts
Uses COMPLIANCE_THRESHOLDS from constants (US-001)
All functions are pure, no DB calls
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)

userStories.title: Unit Tests for Prop Compliance Calculations
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests for the prop compliance utility functions so that we verify correctness of all compliance math.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file created: tests/unit/prop-compliance.test.ts
Tests calculateDrawdownStatus with static, trailing, and EOD scenarios
Tests calculateDailyPnl with multiple trades on same day, no trades
Tests calculateDailyLossStatus safe/caution/danger thresholds
Tests calculateProfitTargetProgress at 0%, 50%, 100%+
Tests calculateConsistencyMetric compliant and non-compliant cases
Tests calculateTradingDays distinct date counting
Tests calculateTrailingDrawdown high-water mark logic with peak tracking
Tests getOverallComplianceStatus returns worst status
All tests pass (bun run test)
Typecheck passes (bun run check)

userStories.title: Create getPropCompliance tRPC Endpoint
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want a single tRPC endpoint that computes all prop compliance metrics for an account so that the frontend can fetch everything in one call.
acceptanceCriteria: Add getPropCompliance query to src/server/api/routers/accounts.ts
Input: { accountId: string }
Validates account ownership and that account is prop_challenge or prop_funded
Fetches all closed trades for the account, sorted by exitTime

Computes and returns: drawdown { current, limit, type, status, equityCurve }, dailyLoss { todayPnl, limit, status }, profitTarget { current, target, progress, status }, consistency { maxDayPercent, limit, isCompliant }, tradingDays { daysTraded, minRequired, remaining, dates }, timeline { startDate, endDate, daysRemaining, daysElapsed }, overallStatus (safe | caution | danger), account basic info
Uses utility functions from US-002
Uses buildEquityCurve() from src/lib/analytics/risk.ts for equity curve
Error constants in src/lib/constants/errors.ts
Uses protectedProcedure for auth
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)

userStories.title: Integration Tests for getPropCompliance Endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests for the prop compliance endpoint so that we verify correct behavior with real database.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file created: tests/integration/prop-compliance.test.ts
Uses setupTrader() fixture to create user + prop_challenge account with all prop fields set
Tests happy path: returns all compliance metrics for account with trades
Tests empty trades: returns safe status, 0% progress
Tests danger state: creates trades that bring account near max drawdown
Tests daily loss limit: creates today's trades exceeding daily limit
Tests profit target progress: creates trades that reach target
Tests auth validation: rejects request for account not owned by user
Tests non-prop account: rejects request for live/demo accounts
Tests trailing drawdown calculation vs static
All tests pass (bun run test)
Typecheck passes (bun run check)

Overall status indicator (safe/caution/danger) with colored dot
Uses Terminal design system: font-mono, text-[10px] labels, border-white/5 cards, rounded borders
Loading skeleton state via DashboardWidget wrapper
Empty state when no prop data available
All data-testid attributes on interactive/key elements

userStories.title: Monte Carlo Challenge Simulator Utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Monte Carlo simulation that models prop challenge outcomes so that we can show X% of simulations pass the challenge.
acceptanceCriteria: Create simulatePropChallenge function in src/lib/analytics/prop-compliance.ts
Inputs: { winRate, avgWin, avgLoss, profitTarget, maxDrawdown, initialBalance, maxTrades, iterations? }

For each simulation: run random trades using win rate and avg win/loss, track equity and high-water-mark drawdown, stop if profit target reached (pass), max drawdown breached (fail), or max trades exceeded (fail)
Returns: { passRate, failRate, avgTradesToPass, medianOutcome, percentiles, simulations }
Default 10000 iterations
Pure function, no DB calls
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)

userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests for the Monte Carlo challenge simulator to verify statistical correctness.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tests to tests/unit/prop-compliance.test.ts (or separate file)
Tests high win rate (80%) produces high pass rate
Tests low win rate (30%) produces low pass rate
Tests edge case: profitTarget = 0 (100% pass)
Tests edge case: maxDrawdown = 0 (100% fail)
Tests return shape: all expected fields present
Tests percentiles are in correct order
All tests pass (bun run test)
